name: Sulista/Gaรบcho Portuguese
notes: Sulista / Gaรบcho (Rio Grande do Sul, Porto Alegre). Conservative non-palatalising default: /t,d/ stay dental [t,d] before /i/ (the encoded default; a generational shift toward palatalisation is in progress and noted). Distinctive alveolar trill/tap onset /r/ [r~ษพ] โ the most European-sounding Brazilian rhotic, NOT glottal; coda /R/ is a tap, not dropped. Reduced final-vowel raising: word-final /e/ often stays [e] in conservative speech. Coda /S/ alveolar. Heterogeneous Italian/German settler substrata. Nascentes (1953:25, O linguajar carioca) places this area in the falares do Sul side of his North/South macro-division (pretonic-vowel openness criterion); Bisol's diphthongization work on /S/-coda (Bisol 1994, 2012), cited in Silva (2014:6-7, ALFAL XVII #4817-4818), draws its impressionistic base specifically from Porto Alegre speech, consistent with the conservative Gaรบcho phonology modelled here, though it targets diphthong structure rather than the /r/ and /t,d/ features asserted by this spec.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortogrรกfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly โ without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. โจoeโฉ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, ยงDitongos).
